---
title: "Project Home"
---

```{r}
shiny_app_url <- Sys.getenv("SHINY_APP_URL", unset = "http://127.0.0.1:3838")
```

::: {.hero-panel}
<div class="tagline">Visual Analytics | Quarto + Shiny | AY2025/26</div>

# Singapore Tourism Time-Series Visual Analytics

## Core Direction
This project reframes Singapore tourism recovery as a comparative time-series problem. The final system combines visual analysis, time-series clustering, and forecasting into one Shiny-supported workflow.

This site is the working base for:

1. Project proposal updates
2. Prototype modules
3. Shiny integration
4. Team coordination and documentation

<div class="quick-links">
<a href="./Proposal/Proposal.html">Open Proposal</a>
<a href="./prototype/module-cluster.html">Open Clustering Prototype</a>
<a href="./app-guide.html">Open Shiny App Page</a>
<a href="`r shiny_app_url`" target="_blank">Launch Shiny App</a>
</div>
:::

## Working Modules

::: {.grid-cards}
::: {.card-block}
#### Time Series Visual Analysis
Compare selected countries and transport modes through raw, indexed, and share-based views.
:::
::: {.card-block}
#### Time Series Clustering
Group country trajectories with similar recovery paths and interpret how a selected priority market sits in the wider recovery landscape.
:::
::: {.card-block}
#### Forecasting
Move from historical comparison into short-term prediction for selected tourism series.
:::
::: {.card-block}
#### Shiny Integration
Use the website as the entry point, then open or embed the running Shiny app from the Shiny page.
:::
:::

## Key Pages

1. [Proposal](./Proposal/Proposal.html)
2. [Time Series Clustering Prototype](./prototype/module-cluster.html)
3. [UI Storyboard](./prototype/ui-storyboard.html)
4. [Shiny App](./app-guide.html)

## Local Run Commands

```bash
quarto preview
```

```bash
Rscript run_app.R 3838
```

## Deployment Notes

The current website render defaults to the local Shiny target:

`r shiny_app_url`

When the app is deployed to `shinyapps.io`, re-render the site with `SHINY_APP_URL` set to the deployed URL so the website button and preview frame point to the public app.
